result.markdown:
--- title: "Proxy network CFR comparison" subtitle: "DoD2k speleothems vs tree-ring baselines" author: "Climate reconstruction project" date: last-modified format: html: toc: true toc-depth: 3 number-sections: true code-fold: false code-tools: true fig-width: 8 fig-height: 5 fig-dpi: 120 params: quick_run: true overwrite_cfr: false execute: warning: false message: false jupyter: dod2k-cfr --- One Quarto notebook: **`{python}`** chunks for DoD2k, **`{r}`** chunks for CFR and tidyverse analysis. Languages meet via a Parquet cache (`output/cache/dod2k_timeseries.parquet`) — no R code calling Python at runtime. ```{r} #| label: setup-r source(file.path( if (file.exists("R/load_project.R")) "." else "proxy-cfr-comparison", "R", "load_project.R" )) load_project() library(tidyverse) library(glue) if (!requireNamespace("arrow", quietly = TRUE)) { stop( "R package 'arrow' is required for the Parquet bridge. Run:\n", " Rscript -e \"install.packages('arrow', repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')\"\n", "If permission errors occur, remove any 00LOCK-arrow in your R library and retry." ) } quick_run <- if (exists("params", inherits = TRUE)) params$quick_run else TRUE overwrite_cfr <- if (exists("params", inherits = TRUE)) params$overwrite_cfr else FALSE ``` # Introduction Compare **climate field reconstructions (CFR)** across proxy networks (DoD2k speleothems vs tree rings). | Step | Language | Task | |------|----------|------| | DoD2k load & filter | Python | [dod2k toolkit](https://lluecke.github.io/dod2k/) | | CFR, validation, plots | R | tidyverse + `Master_CFR.R` | DoD2k distinguishes **archive** (`archiveType`, e.g. Speleothem) from **measurement** (`paleoData_proxy`, e.g. δ18O, δ13C, growth rate, Mg/Ca). CFR below uses speleothem **δ18O** only; the map shows all four speleothem proxy types. **Setup:** `bash scripts/setup_python.sh` (creates `.venv`, installs deps, registers Jupyter kernel `dod2k-cfr`). R needs `arrow` for the Parquet bridge. # 1. DoD2k (Python) ::: {#cell-dod2k-load .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import sys from pathlib import Path for _cand in (Path.cwd(), Path.cwd() / "proxy-cfr-comparison"): _py = _cand / "python" if _py.is_dir() and str(_py) not in sys.path: sys.path.insert(0, str(_py)) from project_env import bootstrap_python_path, data_paths from dod2k_io import load_dod2k, summarize_archives, filter_dod2k, write_dod2k_cache root = bootstrap_python_path() paths = data_paths(root) dod2k_df = load_dod2k(paths["dod2k_csv"]) archive_summary = summarize_archives(dod2k_df) speleo = filter_dod2k(dod2k_df, archive_types=["Speleothem"]) speleo_d18o = filter_dod2k(dod2k_df, archive_types=["Speleothem"], proxy_types=["d18O"]) cache_path = write_dod2k_cache(dod2k_df, paths["cache"]) print(f"Records: {len(dod2k_df):,} | Speleothems (all types): {len(speleo):,} | δ18O: {len(speleo_d18o):,}") print(f"Cache: {cache_path}") archive_summary.head(12) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Records: 4,781 | Speleothems (all types): 567 | δ18O: 224 Cache: /Users/luba/Documents/GitHub/Methods-of-Climate-Reconstruction/proxy-cfr-comparison/output/cache/dod2k_timeseries.parquet ``` ::: ::: {#dod2k-load .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
archiveType paleoData_proxy n
56 Wood ring width 3160
49 Speleothem d18O 224
50 Speleothem growth rate 191
4 Coral d18O 174
51 Wood ARSTAN 173
48 Speleothem d13C 136
7 GlacierIce d18O 126
54 Wood maximum latewood density 109
1 Coral Sr/Ca 98
24 LakeSediment d18O 40
52 Wood d18O 34
31 MarineSediment Mg/Ca 26
``` ::: ::: ## Speleothem proxy types Per [DoD2k documentation](https://lluecke.github.io/dod2k/), speleothems in v2.0 carry four `paleoData_proxy` values (symbols match the official plots). **`n_metadata`** = archive rows; **`n_with_data`** = series with finite values in 0–2000 CE (used on the map). ::: {#cell-speleothem-summary .cell dependson='dod2k-load' execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} from speleothem_viz import speleothem_records, speleothem_summary_table speleo_records = speleothem_records(dod2k_df) speleothem_summary_table(dod2k_df) ``` ::: {#speleothem-summary .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
proxy_label proxy_type n_metadata n_with_data
2 δ18O d18O 224 160
1 δ13C d13C 136 91
3 growth rate growth rate 191 132
0 Mg/Ca Mg/Ca 16 11
``` ::: ::: ::: {#cell-speleothem-map .cell dependson='speleothem-summary' execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} from speleothem_viz import build_speleothem_map map_fig = build_speleothem_map(speleo_records) map_fig.show() ``` ::: {#speleothem-map .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {#cell-speleothem-timeline .cell dependson='speleothem-summary' execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} from speleothem_viz import build_speleothem_timeline timeline_fig = build_speleothem_timeline(speleo_records, max_rows=70) timeline_fig.show() ``` ::: {#speleothem-timeline .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: # 2. Proxy networks (R) ```{r} #| label: preview-networks #| dependson: dod2k-load calib <- CALIBRATION_SCHEMES$course_split$calibration catalog <- read_dod2k_catalog() archive_summary_r <- summarize_dod2k_catalog(catalog) print(archive_summary_r, n = 12) network_ids <- c("dod2k_speleothems", "dod2k_trees", "dod2k_speleo_trees", "ntrend_trees") proxy_map_df <- network_ids |> map(\(id) { build_proxy_network( id, calib_start = calib[1], calib_end = calib[2], catalog = catalog ) |> proxy_network_map_df() |> mutate(label = PROXY_NETWORKS[[id]]$label) }) |> list_rbind() proxy_map_df |> distinct(network, label) |> knitr::kable(caption = "Networks after calibration coverage filter") ``` ```{r} #| label: plot-proxy-locations #| fig-cap: "Proxy locations" #| dependson: preview-networks proxy_map_df |> ggplot(aes(lon, lat, colour = network)) + geom_point(size = 2.2, alpha = 0.85) + borders("world", fill = NA, colour = "grey70") + coord_fixed(1.3) + labs(title = "Proxy networks", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") + theme_minimal() ``` # 3. Run reconstructions (R) ```{r} #| label: select-experiments selected <- if (quick_run) { tribble( ~network_id, ~calibration_id, "dod2k_speleothems", "course_split", "dod2k_speleo_trees", "course_split", "ntrend_trees", "course_split" ) } else { EXPERIMENT_PLAN |> filter(run) |> select(network_id, calibration_id) } selected <- selected |> mutate(experiment_id = str_c(network_id, calibration_id, sep = "__")) knitr::kable(selected, caption = "Experiments to run") ``` ```{r} #| label: run-cfr #| cache: true #| dependson: select-experiments results <- selected |> pmap(\(network_id, calibration_id, experiment_id) { message(glue("=== {experiment_id} ===")) run_cfr_experiment( network_id = network_id, calibration_id = calibration_id, overwrite = overwrite_cfr ) }) |> set_names(selected$experiment_id) ok <- map_lgl(results, \(x) is.null(x$error)) if (!any(ok)) abort("All CFR experiments failed.") if (!all(ok)) { warn(glue("Failed: {paste(names(results)[!ok], collapse = ', ')}")) } ``` # 4. Validation (R) ```{r} #| label: validate-all #| dependson: run-cfr skill_summary <- results[ok] |> map(\(res) validate_cfr_result(res, calibration_id = res$calibration_id)) |> print_skill_summary() knitr::kable(skill_summary, caption = "Skill summary") ``` ```{r} #| label: plot-maps #| fig-height: 6 #| fig-cap: "Validation correlation r" #| dependson: validate-all walk( results[ok], \(res) { skill <- validate_cfr_result(res, calibration_id = res$calibration_id) print( plot_skill_map( skill$grid_validation$r, lon = res$reconstruction$lon, lat = res$reconstruction$lat, title = glue("Validation r — {res$experiment_id}"), limits = c(-1, 1) ) ) } ) ``` # 5. Discussion DoD2k v2.0 contains hundreds of speleothem δ18O records; tree-ring networks give dense NH coverage. Combined networks change the PCA basis of the reconstruction. --- ```{r} #| label: session sessionInfo() ```
